question.text: Within how many days from the end of the quarter must the Investment Manager submit quarterly reports to the Trustee?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under InvIT Regulations, the Investment Manager shall submit quarterly reports on the activities of the InvIT to the trustee within 30 days of the end of the quarter. These include receipts, compliance position, performance report, and status of under-construction projects. **The Trap:** 15 days is the timeline for filing valuation reports (after receipt from valuer) and NAV disclosure to stock exchanges. 45 days and 60 days relate to financial information filing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the key timelines: 30 days for quarterly reports to trustee, 15 days for valuation reports and NAV disclosure, 60 days for annual audit reports to exchanges.
